package.source: CLICK PLC Hardware User Manual, 6th Edition, Rev. Q – C0-USER-M
package.section: DC Input Wiring Methods / DC Output Wiring Methods
package.note: This is a manufacturer manual page illustrating generic DC I/O wiring methods, NOT a project schematic. No IEC 81346 device tags, wire numbers, or cross-references are present. Devices are labelled only by plain-English function.
package.evidence: Title block bottom: 'CLICK PLC Hardware User Manual, 6th Edition, Rev. Q – C0-USER-M  3-31'; header 'Chapter 3: Installation and Wiring'

devices.detail: Generic CLICK PLC DC input point drawn as an opto-coupler with dual (anti-parallel) input diodes so current may flow in either direction (sinking or sourcing). Terminals shown: Input, Common.
devices.evidence: Top-left diagram labelled 'PLC DC Input' with 'Input' and 'Common' terminals and dual-diode opto-coupler symbol; text 'The dual diodes ... allow current to flow in either direction'

devices.detail: Field device with open-collector NPN transistor output; when energized it sinks current to ground from the DC input point. Terminals: Output (sinking), Ground.
devices.evidence: Middle-upper diagram, 'Field Device', label 'DC NPN Sensor (Sinking)', 'Output (sinking)', 'Ground'; text 'open-collector NPN transistor output ... sinks current to ground'

devices.detail: PLC DC input point wired as sourcing; input current is sourced from the Common terminal connected to power supply (+). Terminals: Input (sourcing), Common.
devices.evidence: Middle-upper diagram 'PLC DC Input', 'Input (sourcing)', 'Common', callout 'PLC Input (Sourcing)'; text 'PLC input current is sourced from the common terminal connected to power supply (+)'

devices.detail: DC supply (battery symbol with + terminal) providing loop power between PLC Common (+) and sensor Ground for the NPN sinking-sensor to PLC sourcing-input example.
devices.evidence: Middle-upper diagram center, label 'Supply' with battery symbol and '+' mark

devices.detail: Field device with open-emitter PNP transistor output; when energized it sources current to the PLC input point. Terminals: Output (sourcing), Ground.
devices.evidence: Middle-lower diagram 'Field Device', label 'DC PNP Sensor (Sourcing)', 'Output (sourcing)', 'Ground'; text 'open-emitter PNP transistor output ... sources current to the PLC input point'

devices.detail: PLC DC input point wired as sinking; it sinks the current supplied by the PNP sensor to ground. No additional module power supply required since the field-device loop is sourcing. Terminals: Input (sinking), Common.
devices.evidence: Middle-lower diagram 'PLC DC Input', 'Input (sinking)', 'Common', callout 'PLC Input (Sinking)'; text 'PLC input point, which sinks the current to ground'

devices.detail: CLICK PLC DC output point drawn with a transistor/MOSFET; output sinks current to ground (Common) when energized. Terminals: Power (+DC Power), Output (sinking), Common.
devices.evidence: Bottom diagram 'PLC DC Output', '+DC Power', 'Power', 'Output (sinking)', 'Common', MOSFET symbol; text 'the PLC output point sinks current to ground (common) when energized'

devices.type: field device load with sourcing input
devices.detail: Load device with a sourcing input driven by the PLC sinking output. Terminals: Input (sourcing), Ground.
devices.evidence: Bottom-right diagram 'Field Device', 'Input (sourcing)', 'Ground'; text 'output is connected to a field device load with a sourcing input'

devices.detail: DC supply rated 20-28 VDC (battery symbol with +) powering the PLC sinking-output to sourcing-load loop.
devices.evidence: Bottom diagram center, battery symbol labelled '20-28 VDC' with '+'

cross_sheet_notes: Single manual page (3-31). Four generic wiring examples: (1) generic PLC DC input opto-coupler; (2) NPN sinking sensor to PLC sourcing input with external Supply; (3) PNP sourcing sensor to PLC sinking input (no extra module supply needed); (4) PLC sinking DC output to a sourcing-input field-device load with a 20-28 VDC supply. No off-page cross-references.

brief.sheet_title: Manual page 3-31 (CLICK PLC Hardware User Manual, Ch. 3) showing generic DC input and DC output wiring methods between PLC I/O points and field devices.
brief.purpose: This page teaches how to wire CLICK PLC DC I/O to field devices, not a specific machine. The DC input point is drawn as an opto-coupler with dual (anti-parallel) diodes, so it can be wired either sinking or sourcing. Example 1: an NPN (open-collector) sensor that sinks current is paired with a PLC input wired sourcing, with an external DC supply feeding the Common terminal. Example 2: a PNP (open-emitter) sensor that sources current is paired with a PLC input wired sinking, and no extra module supply is needed because the field loop already sources current. Example 3 (output side): a PLC DC output wired sinking drives a field-device load with a sourcing input, powered by a 20-28 VDC supply. Stated ranges: DC inputs ~+12-24 VDC, DC outputs ~+5-24 VDC.

key_signals.signal: NPN sensor output signal into the PLC (sensor sinks, PLC sources)
PNP sensor output signal into the PLC (sensor sources, PLC sinks)
PLC output signal to the field-device load (PLC sinks to Common)
Supply power to PLC Common / field loop (input examples)
20-28 VDC loop power for the output example
key_signals.destination: PLC DC Output and field-device load loop

key_devices.device: Generic PLC DC input point (dual-diode opto-coupler, wireable sinking or sourcing)
Field-device load with a sourcing input
External DC supply for input loop

brief.troubleshooting_example: This is an instructional page with no tagged terminals, so use it as method guidance rather than a point-to-point map. For the NPN-sensor / sourcing-input example: with the sensor commanded ON, measure across the PLC 'Input' vs 'Common' terminals — if the input point does not activate, first meter voltage at the sensor 'Output (sinking)' terminal referenced to the loop ground: if the sensor output does not pull toward ground/Common when energized, the sensor (device) is at fault; if the sensor output IS switching correctly but the same signal is absent at the PLC 'Input' terminal, the fault is the conductor/connection between sensor Output and PLC Input. Note: the actual PLC program/logic response is NOT shown on this page, so do not assume what the controller does with the input.
brief.safety_context: Voltages printed on this page: DC inputs typically +12-24 VDC, DC outputs typically +5-24 VDC, and the output example uses a 20-28 VDC supply — no higher/AC levels are shown, and this is a generic reference, not proof of your actual field conditions. For continuity/resistance checks on wiring, de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. For live signal/voltage checks on the low-voltage DC loops, use appropriately rated meters and follow energized-work procedures. The drawing does not establish the real installed voltage of your specific panel.
unresolved_items: No device tags, wire numbers, terminal numbers, or PLC channel addresses are printed — this is a generic method illustration.
No specific CLICK module part number is identified for the illustrated I/O points.
